publicFooter.defaultSummary: I build business systems that feel calm, fast, and useful.
publicFooter.copyright: Crafted for recruiters, hiring managers, and product teams.

homePage.identityErrorTitle: Portfolio identity could not be loaded.
homePage.identityErrorDescription: Please make sure the API is running and the public portfolio endpoints are available.
homePage.detailsWarningTitle: Identity loaded. Extra homepage sections are temporarily unavailable.
homePage.detailsWarningDescription: The hero is still using the live identity endpoint while the rest of the homepage waits for the content endpoint.
homePage.strategyCardTitle: Designed for recruiters, technical leads, and clients who want proof, not buzzwords.
homePage.strategyCardSummary: The homepage leads with real backend content, clear navigation, and focused project signals instead of decorative filler.
homePage.spotlightFallback: Homepage details are loading, but the positioning and CTA flow are already live.
homePage.techTitle: Modern frontend, reliable backend, and product-shaped architecture
homePage.techDescription: Each card reflects a real delivery area used in the existing API contracts and project content.
homePage.featuredTitle: Case studies built around systems, modules, and business outcomes
homePage.featuredDescription: These highlights make the .NET + Angular specialization obvious while staying readable for hiring and freelance conversations.
homePage.erpTitle: Enterprise workflow thinking backed by implementation detail
homePage.erpDescription: The ERP section ties together backend rules, Angular screens, reporting needs, and the case-study proof already exposed by the API.
homePage.businessTitle: Why this portfolio is positioned around outcomes, not just tooling
homePage.businessDescription: The value section turns technical strengths into the language recruiters and clients actually evaluate.

experiencePage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the public experience endpoint is reachable.
experiencePage.highlightsTitle: Short proof points instead of a long biography
experiencePage.highlightsDescription: The page is designed to stay scannable for recruiters while still showing how analytical thinking, structured training, and ERP delivery connect.
experiencePage.timelineTitle: A concise path from foundations to ERP delivery
experiencePage.timelineDescription: Each stage keeps the story short, practical, and focused on transferable value rather than long narrative text.
experiencePage.fallbackHeadline: Full-stack delivery shaped by business systems thinking
experiencePage.fallbackSummary: This page explains the journey behind the portfolio: analytical foundations, practical .NET training, and ERP-oriented implementation work.
experiencePage.aboutLead: My path runs from an engineering foundation, through analytical quality-focused work, into full-stack .NET development. Today I build real business systems end to end — not just screens.
experiencePage.scBodyFallback: Business-oriented .NET Full Stack Developer focused on ERP workflows, secure APIs, and admin dashboards. I turn business requirements into clean, maintainable systems.
experiencePage.expSectionTitle: A clear progression toward business software
experiencePage.expSectionSub: Each step added something I still use — structured thinking, analytical rigor, and modern full-stack delivery.
experiencePage.ctaDescription: See the case studies behind the experience, or start a conversation about your next system.

skillsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the public skills endpoint is reachable.
skillsPage.title: Structured strengths across the full stack
skillsPage.summary: This page keeps the stack easy to scan for recruiters and clients: primary delivery skills stand out first, while supporting capabilities stay visible without crowding the layout.
skillsPage.featuredTitle: Core tools and patterns used in real portfolio delivery
skillsPage.featuredDescription: These highlighted skills come directly from the live backend seed and make the .NET, Angular, SQL Server, and architecture strengths immediately obvious.
skillsPage.matrixTitle: Grouped by delivery area, not presented as a crowded tag wall
skillsPage.matrixDescription: Each card keeps primary skills visually stronger than the supporting layer while preserving a calm, responsive reading rhythm on mobile, tablet, laptop, and standard desktop screens.

projectsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the public projects endpoint is reachable.
projectsPage.warningTitle: Projects could not be refreshed right now.
projectsPage.warningDescription: The latest visible results are still shown while the next request is unavailable.
projectsPage.title: Business-shaped project stories with clear technical proof
projectsPage.summary: This page presents live portfolio projects as business case studies, with concise summaries, value-focused framing, and filter controls built for recruiters, clients, and technical reviewers.
projectsPage.signalTheme: Dark, light, LTR, and RTL ready
projectsPage.emptyFeaturedDescription: As soon as the live project list is available, the strongest featured case study appears here automatically.
projectsPage.filtersTitle: Find the right project angle quickly
projectsPage.filtersDescription: Filter by project type or technology to move from broad portfolio review to specific implementation examples without losing the business context.
projectsPage.noActiveFilters: No active filters yet. Start with project type or technology to narrow the list.
projectsPage.emptyDescription: Try clearing the filters to return to the full portfolio list.

projectDetailsPage.errorTitle: This case study is temporarily unavailable.
projectDetailsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the project details endpoint is reachable.
projectDetailsPage.notFoundDescription: The requested project slug does not map to an active case study right now.
projectDetailsPage.heroSignalTheme: Dark, light, LTR, and RTL ready
projectDetailsPage.storySignalScope: Database scope shaped for real portfolio content
projectDetailsPage.storySignalCodeFirst: EF Core Code First with ABP conventions
projectDetailsPage.storySignalReady: Existing endpoints stay ready for Angular 21
projectDetailsPage.highlightsTitle: Proof points recruiters and technical reviewers can scan quickly
projectDetailsPage.highlightsDescription: These cards summarize the strongest implementation angles without making the page feel heavy.
projectDetailsPage.databaseSpotlightTitle: A clean entity model that supports public content, contact flows, and future admin work
projectDetailsPage.databaseSpotlightDescription: This story emphasizes how the backend owns schema quality, DTO boundaries, and seed-ready SQL Server persistence while the frontend keeps a modern presentation layer.
projectDetailsPage.apiSpotlightTitle: Public endpoints that keep the frontend live while the backend keeps ownership
projectDetailsPage.apiSpotlightDescription: This story turns the portfolio site into a real API consumer: Angular stays focused on polished UX while the backend owns anonymous access rules, DTO shape, validation, and contact persistence.
projectDetailsPage.erpSpotlightTitle: A stronger read on architecture, permissions, and workflow design
projectDetailsPage.erpSpotlightDescription: This ERP case study gets extra structure because it is the clearest proof of full-stack delivery across Angular screens, backend rules, reporting, and secure business workflows.
projectDetailsPage.workflowTitle: Workflow-aware modules and protected business actions
projectDetailsPage.apiEndpointsTitle: Public endpoints that power the portfolio experience
projectDetailsPage.architectureTitle: Backend-first architecture that keeps the UI presentation-focused
projectDetailsPage.databaseStandardsTitle: Shared fields, persistence rules, and backend ownership
projectDetailsPage.apiRulesTitle: Public API rules that stay owned by the backend
projectDetailsPage.galleryTitle: Visual placeholders stay supported until project screenshots are added
projectDetailsPage.galleryDescription: The page renders real gallery items from the backend whether they are screenshots or placeholder slots for future visuals.
projectDetailsPage.databaseAcceptanceTitle: What this story needed to satisfy before it was done
projectDetailsPage.apiAcceptanceTitle: What Story 4.3 needed to satisfy before it was done
projectDetailsPage.linksTitle: Use live links when they exist
projectDetailsPage.linksDescription: GitHub and live-demo actions only appear when the backend exposes them for the current project.
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveTitle: Real API surfaces the page can verify right now
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveDescription: These cards are built from the existing public endpoints already wired into the app, so the design showcases real data instead of mock payloads.
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveErrorTitle: Live endpoint preview is temporarily unavailable.
projectDetailsPage.apiLiveErrorDescription: The case study still renders, but the preview cards need the backend API to be reachable.
projectDetailsPage.apiProjectsCardSummary: The list endpoint provides live recruiter-facing cards plus project-type filter metadata for the projects page.
projectDetailsPage.apiProjectDetailsCardSummary: This case-study page itself is loaded from the slug endpoint, proving the public details contract in the browser.
projectDetailsPage.apiSkillsCardSummary: The skills endpoint returns categorized groups so the public skills page stays readable and backend-driven.
projectDetailsPage.apiExperienceCardSummary: The experience endpoint keeps the about page backed by timeline items and highlight badges from the backend.
projectDetailsPage.apiSettingsCardSummary: The settings endpoint exposes presentation-supporting values without hardcoding them into Angular.
projectDetailsPage.apiContactCardSummary: The contact endpoint accepts real form submissions, validates them on the backend, and saves them for later review.
projectDetailsPage.apiContactCardCaption: required fields, email validation, and spam honeypot

contactPage.title: A calm, modern way to start the next full-stack conversation
contactPage.summary: This contact page is built for recruiters, clients, and product teams who want a fast path to reach out. It stays simple, responsive, bilingual, and theme-ready while sending real submissions to the existing backend.
contactPage.signalBilingual: English LTR first, Arabic RTL ready
contactPage.storyTitle: Story 6.1 is expressed as a real lead-capture surface
contactPage.storyDescription: The UI keeps the conversation focused and easy to scan, while the backend remains responsible for validation, spam protection, and message persistence.
contactPage.promiseValidationDescription: Name, email, subject, and message stay validated so the form feels guided instead of vague.
contactPage.promiseStorageDescription: Successful submissions return a real backend response and are stored for later review in the admin flow.
contactPage.promiseSpamDescription: The public form includes the existing honeypot field so automated noise can be filtered without hurting UX.
contactPage.siteSettingsEndpointSummary: Loads branded public settings for presentation support.
contactPage.contactEndpointSummary: Submits validated contact messages to the backend database.
contactPage.formTitle: Share the role, product, or workflow you want to discuss
contactPage.formDescription: Keep the brief concise and practical. The form is designed to feel lightweight on mobile, tablet, laptop, and standard desktop screens while still showing clear validation and submission states.
contactPage.messagePlaceholder: Tell me about the role, project scope, or business problem you want to solve.
contactPage.backendNote: Submission is validated and saved by the backend API, not simulated in the browser.
contactPage.successDescription: The contact request has been accepted by the backend and is ready for follow-up.
contactPage.errorTitle: The message could not be submitted.
contactPage.errorDescription: The request could not be completed. Please make sure the backend API is running and try again.
contactPage.processDescription: The page keeps the process visible so visitors know what this form is for and why it is reliable.
contactPage.processStepCaptureDescription: Visitors share the opportunity, project scope, or business problem in a short structured format.
contactPage.processStepReviewDescription: The backend checks required fields, email format, and the honeypot before saving the message.
contactPage.processStepResponseDescription: Once stored, the message can move into the admin inbox and the broader portfolio follow-up workflow.
contactPage.linksTitle: Live professional routes stay connected to the public portfolio
contactPage.linksDescription: These links come from the existing public home-page contract, so this page stays integrated with the same backend-driven profile content.
contactPage.settingsTitle: Public settings loaded from the API
contactPage.settingsDescription: These values come from `/api/site-settings`, keeping presentation support data in the existing backend instead of hardcoded frontend placeholders.
contactPage.validationMinLength: Please add a little more detail.
contactPage.validationDisposableEmail: Please use a real work or personal email.

adminSidebar.caption: Admin workspace
adminSidebar.helper: Protected operations for projects, skills, experience, and messages.

adminLoginPage.title: Admin authentication for a modern full-stack portfolio.
adminLoginPage.description: Sign in through the real backend JWT flow to manage projects, skills, experience, and messages from a clean responsive control area.
adminLoginPage.highlightsTitle: Built around the backlog acceptance criteria
adminLoginPage.formDescription: The frontend keeps the form focused and polished while the backend remains the source of truth for credentials, permissions, JWT issuance, and localized errors.
securityPoints: Only authenticated admin users can enter the protected portfolio workspace.
The login experience stays clear on phone, tablet, laptop, and standard desktop screens.
Language and theme controls stay consistent across English LTR and Arabic RTL views.
adminLoginPage.endpointNote: This screen signs in through the existing `/api/admin/authentication/login` endpoint and relies on the Angular interceptor to attach the bearer token to protected admin requests.
adminLoginPage.errorFallback: The admin sign-in request failed. Please review your credentials and try again.
adminLoginPage.networkError: The backend API could not be reached. Please make sure the server is running and try again.
adminLoginPage.validationUserNameMaxLength: Username or email must be 256 characters or fewer.

adminDashboardPage.loadingDescription: The dashboard is requesting the live admin overview endpoint and preparing the latest portfolio signals.
adminDashboardPage.errorTitle: The admin overview is temporarily unavailable.
adminDashboardPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the backend API is running and the protected dashboard endpoint is reachable.
adminDashboardPage.warningTitle: The latest refresh could not be completed.
adminDashboardPage.warningDescription: The most recent successful dashboard data is still visible while the next request is unavailable.
adminDashboardPage.title: A calm live control room for a modern full-stack developer portfolio.
adminDashboardPage.summary: This overview keeps the admin area simple, polished, and useful: real metrics, quick routes into management screens, and recent message visibility through the existing protected backend endpoint.
adminDashboardPage.signalTheme: Dark, light, LTR, and RTL ready
adminDashboardPage.overviewTitle: Overview built from the existing admin API, not placeholder aggregation.
adminDashboardPage.overviewDescription: The screen is localized, direction-aware, and theme-aware while the backend remains the source of truth for permissions, counts, recent inbox activity, and available admin routes.
adminDashboardPage.metricProjectsContext: Portfolio projects currently available for admin review and publishing work.
adminDashboardPage.metricSkillsContext: Structured stack entries ready to support the recruiter-facing skills matrix.
adminDashboardPage.metricMessagesContext: Contact messages captured by the real backend flow and available for follow-up.
adminDashboardPage.metricUnreadContext: Unread conversations that still need review inside the admin workspace.
adminDashboardPage.metricFallbackContext: Live dashboard metric exposed by the protected backend overview contract.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionsTitle: Quick actions to the screens that move portfolio content forward
adminDashboardPage.quickActionsDescription: These routes come directly from the authorized admin shell definition, so only permitted sections appear here.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionsEmptyTitle: No quick actions are currently available.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionsEmptyDescription: When the backend exposes authorized admin routes, they will appear here automatically.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionProjects: Review portfolio entries, publishing readiness, and case-study coverage.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionSkills: Refine the skills matrix that supports the public full-stack positioning.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionExperience: Update the professional timeline and business-value framing for recruiters.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionMessages: Triage new conversations and respond through your normal workflow tools.
adminDashboardPage.quickActionFallback: Open the next authorized admin section exposed by the live app shell.
adminDashboardPage.messagesTitle: Latest contact messages visible from the protected dashboard contract
adminDashboardPage.messagesDescription: Recent messages stay concise here so the dashboard remains quick to scan on phone, tablet, laptop, and standard desktop screens.
adminDashboardPage.messagesEmptyDescription: As soon as the backend has inbox activity, recent contact entries will appear here.

adminProjectsPage.title: Manage portfolio projects with live admin APIs and a calm, responsive workflow.
adminProjectsPage.summary: This screen turns the existing admin projects endpoints into a modern CRUD workspace with publish, feature, search, and case-study editing support across English LTR and Arabic RTL.
adminProjectsPage.tableTitle: Projects table with sensible widths and real CRUD actions
adminProjectsPage.tableDescription: Search, filter, publish, feature, and open a full editor without leaving the admin workspace.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Search title, slug, summary, value, or technology

adminProjectsPage.warningTitle: The latest refresh could not be completed.
adminProjectsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the protected admin projects endpoint is reachable and try again.
adminProjectsPage.emptyDescription: Clear the current filters or create a new project to continue.

editor.emptyTitle: Choose a project or start a new one.
editor.emptyDescription: The editor uses the real protected project endpoints, so changes here update the live backend content model instead of local placeholder data.
editor.createTitle: Add a new business case study
editor.createDescription: Fill the core project details first, then add the case-study sections, highlights, and gallery entries the public portfolio needs.
editor.editDescription: Update publishing state, featured placement, business summary, and full case-study content from one responsive workspace.
editor.errorTitle: Project details could not be loaded.
editor.errorDescription: Please make sure the admin project details endpoint is reachable and try again.

sections.basicsHint: Core identity, ordering, and publication controls.
sections.summaryHint: Keep the recruiter-facing summary concise while preserving business value context.
sections.linksHint: Use live GitHub and demo links only when they actually exist.
sections.caseStudyHint: These sections drive the public project details page and should stay practical and easy to scan.
sections.highlightsHint: Use a few concise proof cards for the strongest delivery points.
sections.galleryHint: Support screenshots now and placeholder slots for future visuals.

hints.isActive: Unpublished projects stay available in admin but hidden from the public portfolio.
hints.isFeatured: Featured projects can be promoted on the homepage and in portfolio highlights.
hints.techStack: Add one technology per line so the backend receives a clean list.
hints.optionalMultiline: Optional. Add one item per line when needed.

deleteConfirmation.title: Delete this project?
deleteConfirmation.description: This removes the project from the admin list and public content flow. Please confirm before continuing.

messages.saveError: The project could not be saved.
messages.deleteError: The project could not be deleted.
messages.publicationError: The publication status could not be updated.
messages.featureError: The featured status could not be updated.

validation.url: Enter a valid URL that starts with http or https.

adminSkillsPage.title: Manage portfolio skills through a modern, lightweight admin workspace.
adminSkillsPage.summary: This screen uses the live protected skills endpoints for real CRUD, category ordering, primary/secondary emphasis, and active visibility control across English LTR and Arabic RTL.
adminSkillsPage.tableTitle: A focused CRUD workspace for technical and business capabilities
adminSkillsPage.tableDescription: Search, filter, reorder, and tune public visibility without leaving the admin area.
adminSkillsPage.errorDescription: Please make sure the protected admin skills endpoint is reachable and try again.
adminSkillsPage.emptyDescription: Create the first skill to start shaping the public portfolio matrix.
adminSkillsPage.emptyFilteredDescription: Adjust the filters or clear them to see the full list again.

editor.emptyTitle: Choose a skill or create a new one.
editor.emptyDescription: The editor writes directly to the backend-owned skills model, so the public portfolio reflects real content instead of placeholders.
editor.createDescription: Add the skill name, place it in the right category, then decide whether it should be primary and public.
editor.editDescription: Fine-tune ordering, emphasis, and visibility while keeping the portfolio matrix clear and recruiter-friendly.
editor.errorTitle: Skill details could not be loaded.
editor.errorDescription: Please make sure the admin skill details endpoint is reachable and try again.

sections.basicsHint: Name, category, and ordering stay backend-owned and public-site ready.
sections.visibilityHint: Use these toggles to control whether a skill appears publicly and whether it should be visually emphasized.

hints.isPrimary: Primary skills receive stronger visual weight on the public portfolio.
hints.isActive: Inactive skills stay manageable in admin but are hidden from visitors.

deleteConfirmation.title: Delete this skill?
deleteConfirmation.description: This removes the skill from the admin catalog and the public portfolio output. Please confirm before continuing.

messages.saveError: The skill could not be saved.
messages.deleteError: The skill could not be deleted.
messages.quickUpdateError: The skill status could not be updated.

adminExperiencePage.title: Shape the portfolio timeline with a polished admin experience powered by real APIs.
adminExperiencePage.summary: This screen manages the live experience timeline through the protected admin experience endpoints, including CRUD, ordering, primary emphasis, and public visibility across light/dark themes and bilingual layouts.
adminExperiencePage.tableTitle: A clear editor for career stages, business value, and public proof points
adminExperiencePage.tableDescription: Keep the public timeline practical and easy to scan while maintaining real backend content.

filters.searchPlaceholder: Search title, organization, stage, type, or summary

adminExperiencePage.errorDescription: Please make sure the protected admin experience endpoint is reachable and try again.
adminExperiencePage.emptyTitle: No experience items are available yet.
adminExperiencePage.emptyDescription: Create the first timeline item to start shaping the public experience story.
adminExperiencePage.emptyFilteredTitle: No experience items match these filters.
adminExperiencePage.emptyFilteredDescription: Adjust the filters or clear them to see the full timeline again.

editor.emptyTitle: Choose a timeline item or create a new one.
editor.emptyDescription: The editor updates the backend-owned experience timeline directly so the public page always reflects real content.
editor.createDescription: Capture the stage label, role framing, organization, business value, and supporting proof in one place.
editor.editDescription: Tune the public story, highlight bullets, primary emphasis, and visibility without leaving the admin workspace.
editor.errorTitle: Experience details could not be loaded.
editor.errorDescription: Please make sure the admin experience details endpoint is reachable and try again.

sections.identityHint: Define the type, stage, title, organization, and public order.
sections.storyHint: Keep the public story concise, business-aware, and easy to scan.
sections.visibilityHint: Control whether this item appears publicly and whether it should carry stronger prominence.

placeholders.title: Enterprise ERP Delivery
placeholders.summary: Short public summary of this stage.
placeholders.businessValue: What this stage proves for recruiters, clients, or hiring teams.

hints.highlights: Add one proof point per line.
hints.isPrimary: Use this for the strongest proof of professional software delivery.
hints.isActive: Inactive items stay editable in admin but do not appear on the public timeline.

deleteConfirmation.description: This removes the experience item from the admin timeline and public portfolio output. Please confirm before continuing.
